title: Curator of the Budget
description: The Curator sits at a folding table in the strip mall office (between the dry cleaner and the frozen yogurt place), surrounded by binders. Requests receipts for everything.

lines: The office was wedged between the dry cleaner and the frozen yogurt place in Maple Heights' only strip mall. It smelled like toner and decisions made by committee.
At the folding table in the center sat a figure surrounded by binders. Color-coded binders. Eighteen of them. The figure was doing math on a legal pad and did not look up when the party entered.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Budget
lines.text: Let me see your receipts. All of them.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: We don't have receipts. We came through a portal.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Budget
lines.text: The portal is a line item. I need to know what it cost and who approved it.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Budget
lines.text: Unauthorized expenditures are still expenditures. They go in the variance column. The variance column is my least favorite column.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Budget
lines.text: Your weapons — cost? Your armor — source? Your potions — vendor? Your travel from the previous world — mileage reimbursement or flat rate?

lines.speaker: Curator of the Budget
lines.text: Looted items carry acquisition cost zero but depreciation begins at point of combat. Do you have the dates?

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: I genuinely cannot tell if you're going to fight us or audit us.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Budget
lines.text: Both. The audit is a prerequisite. I need to know what you have before I can correctly quantify what I am taking.
